cta.heading: Book a boiler repair call-out
cta.text: If the failed part is affecting heating, hot water or boiler pressure, we can diagnose the fault and advise on repair versus replacement.

faq.question: Can this part always be repaired instead of replaced?
faq.answer: Not always. Some parts can be adjusted or re-pressurised, but many failed components need replacing once they are confirmed faulty.

faq.question: Will replacing one part solve the whole issue?
faq.answer: Often it will, but the right answer depends on proper diagnosis. Symptoms like low pressure or no hot water can be caused by more than one component.

faq.question: Should I book an engineer if the boiler still works sometimes?
faq.answer: Yes. Intermittent faults usually point to a part that is deteriorating and can lead to a full breakdown if ignored.
